data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Highlights Strengthening Dollar Dominance
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant has stated that the dominance of the U.S. dollar and its status as a reserve currency have further strengthened. According to ChainCatcher, this assertion underscores the ongoing influence of the dollar in global financial markets.

data.title: U.S. Consumer Confidence Drops Amid Iran Conflict Concerns
data.description:
On April 24, Jin10 reported that a survey released on Friday indicated a decline in U.S. consumer confidence in April, reaching its lowest level in nearly four years due to inflation concerns exacerbated by the Iran conflict. According to Jin10, the University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center stated that its consumer confidence index's final reading for the month was 49.8, marking the lowest point since June 2022. However, this figure showed an improvement from the earlier April reading of 47.6. In March, the index stood at 53.3.

"Following the announcement of a two-week ceasefire agreement and a slight decrease in gasoline prices, consumer confidence has partially recovered from the early month's decline," said Joanne Hsu, director of the Consumer Survey Center. "The Iran conflict appears to impact consumer perceptions primarily through its effects on gasoline prices and other potential costs. In contrast, military and diplomatic developments that fail to alleviate supply constraints or reduce energy prices are unlikely to boost consumer confidence."

data.title: Ameriprise Data Breach Affects Thousands, Exposing Sensitive Information
data.description: Ameriprise has revealed that a data breach in March impacted 47,876 individuals, potentially compromising their personal and financial information. According to NS3.AI, the company identified the breach on March 18. Additionally, Ameriprise reported a separate phishing incident in December, which affected 598 people.

data.title: Kalibaf Dismisses Resignation Rumors
data.description: Aiman Shamsaei has clarified that Kalibaf has not resigned and continues to perform his responsibilities. According to NS3.AI, Shamsaei stated that the rumors regarding Kalibaf's resignation were circulated to mislead public opinion.

data.title: U.S. Department of Justice to End Investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is anticipated to conclude its criminal investigation concerning Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. According to NS3.AI, Powell has been identified as the subject of this investigation. The decision to drop the investigation marks a significant development in the scrutiny surrounding Powell's actions and decisions during his tenure. The outcome may influence perceptions of the Federal Reserve's leadership and its ongoing policies.

data.title: The Justice Department is expected to drop the criminal investigation against Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell.
data.description: ABC News: The Justice Department is expected to drop the criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell.

data.title: China and Indonesia to Launch QRIS Cross-Border Payments on April 30
data.description: China and Indonesia have announced the official launch of QRIS cross-border payments starting April 30, following a successful trial that processed 1.64 million transactions valued at Rp 556 billion ($32 million). According to NS3.AI, Bank Indonesia stated that the system will enable travelers in both countries to use compatible payment apps, reducing reliance on U.S. dollars. BI Deputy Governor Filianingsih Hendarta confirmed that the technical and business aspects are prepared for implementation.

data.title: Strategy Surpasses BlackRock as Largest Institutional Bitcoin Holder
data.description: Strategy announced this week that it now holds over 800,000 Bitcoin following a $2.5 billion acquisition of 34,000 Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, Michael Saylor, the company's executive chairman, stated that the 'bitcoin winter' has ended, as Bitcoin has rebounded 30% from its recent lows of $60,000. This purchase positions Strategy ahead of BlackRock's IBIT fund, marking the first time since the second quarter of 2024 that Strategy has become the largest institutional Bitcoin holder. Benoît Bosc from x2B commented that the rebound is more indicative of macro liquidity trends rather than crypto-specific strength.

data.title: Iranian Official Denies Resignation of Negotiation Leader
data.description:
Iranian Parliament's Communication, Media, and Cultural Affairs Center head, Ayman Shamsaei, has refuted claims regarding the resignation of the negotiation leader, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. According to Odaily, Shamsaei stated that Ghalibaf has not resigned and continues to fulfill his duties diligently. The rumors were spread with the intent to disrupt public opinion.

Earlier reports from Iranian media suggested that Ghalibaf, who was leading the Iranian delegation in talks with the United States in Islamabad, had resigned and withdrawn from the negotiation team.

data.title: Reliance Industries' Q4 Net Profit Surpasses Expectations
data.description: Reliance Industries reported a net profit of 169.7 billion rupees for the fourth quarter, exceeding the anticipated 169.43 billion rupees. According to Jin10, the company's performance was stronger than analysts had forecasted, reflecting its robust financial health. The results highlight Reliance Industries' ability to outperform market expectations, contributing to its reputation as a leading player in the industry.

data.title: University of Michigan's April Current Conditions Index Revised Upward
data.description: The University of Michigan's Current Conditions Index for April was revised to a final value of 52.5, surpassing the expected 50.7, according to Jin10. The previous value was recorded at 50.1.

data.title: U.S. April Inflation Rate Expectations Revised to 3.5%
data.description: The final estimate for the U.S. inflation rate expectations for the five to ten-year period in April was revised to 3.5%, according to Jin10. This figure was higher than the anticipated 3.40% and the previous value of 3.40%.

data.title: Iranian media: The head of the negotiating delegation has not resigned; related rumors are being spread for the purpose of disrupting public opinion.
data.description: According to Iran's International Television, Ayman Shamsaye, head of the Iranian Parliament's Center for Communications, Media and Cultural Affairs, responded to reports of Ghalibaf's resignation, stating that Ghalibaf has not resigned and is still diligently fulfilling his duties. He added that the rumors were spread for the purpose of misleading public opinion. Earlier reports from Iranian media indicated that Ghalibaf, the head of the Iranian delegation negotiating with the United States in Islamabad, had resigned and withdrawn from the negotiating team.

data.title: Zondacrypto CEO Faces Investigation Amid Alleged Fraud and Missing Bitcoin
data.description: Polish prosecutors are currently investigating Zondacrypto over allegations of fraud and potential losses amounting to at least 350 million zloty. According to NS3.AI, reports have emerged that CEO Przemyslaw Kral has traveled to Israel amid the ongoing investigation. Last week, Kral disclosed that Zondacrypto was unable to access a cold wallet containing 4,500 Bitcoin. Former board member Georgi Dzaniašvili revealed that supervisors became aware of the crisis's magnitude through media reports rather than internal communications.

data.title: Web3 Gaming Projects Face Decline as Funding Drops
data.description: Caladan reports that 93% of Web3 gaming projects have become inactive following a ChainPlay review of over 3,200 GameFi titles. According to NS3.AI, Caladan estimates that approximately $12 billion has been invested in these projects since 2020. However, the average token prices have plummeted by 95% from their peaks in 2022. Additionally, annual funding has significantly decreased from $4 billion in 2022 to around $360 million in 2025.

data.title: Airlines Reduce Flights as Jet Fuel Prices Surge
data.description: Airlines are reducing their flight schedules following the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which has caused jet fuel prices to more than double. According to NS3.AI, Lufthansa Group announced it will cancel 20,000 short-haul flights through October. Domestic airfare has increased by approximately 18% compared to last year, translating to around $55 more per trip. IEA Director Fatih Birol has warned that Europe has about six weeks of jet fuel stocks remaining.

data.title: China-Linked Cyber Threats Exploit Botnets for Global Attacks
data.description: The National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC), along with 15 international partners, has issued a joint advisory warning about cyber threats linked to China. According to NS3.AI, these threat actors are using botnets composed of compromised internet devices to conceal their attacks. One such botnet, known as Raptor Train, reportedly infected over 200,000 devices globally in 2024. The advisory also references Department of Justice filings, which indicate that the group Volt Typhoon has established footholds in critical infrastructure across the United States and allied nations.

data.title: Abraxas Capital deposited 4,835 BTC, worth $378 million, into Kraken.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Abraxas Capital (abra...unds) deposited 4,835 Bitcoins, worth $378 million, into Kraken in the past hour. Additionally, the firm deposited 6,000 XAUTs, worth $28 million, into Binance, Bybit, OKX, and Bitfinex.

data.title: An address suspected to belong to the LAB team transferred 100 million tokens to three Bitget deposit addresses, representing 43.4% of the total circulating supply.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, LAB core-related addresses recently made concentrated fund transfers to three Bitget deposit addresses (0xDd7..., 0x659..., 0xb11...). Statistics show that these three addresses received a total of 100 million LAB tokens, worth approximately $64.96 million USD, which were subsequently deposited into Bitget hot wallets. Currently, the circulating supply of LAB is 230.4M, and the total amount received by these three addresses accounts for approximately 43.4% of its total circulating supply. LAB is currently trading at $0.6496, down 0.74% in the last 24 hours, with a circulating market capitalization of approximately $149 million. A single aggregation and deposit of over 40% of the circulating shares may indicate a large-scale sell-off or adjustments by market makers.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Market Opens Higher with Tech Stocks Leading Gains
data.description: On April 24, U.S. stock markets opened with gains as the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by 0.1%, the S&P 500 increased by 0.38%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed over 0.6%. According to Jin10, Intel (INTC.O) surged 24%, reaching a record high after its first-quarter earnings and second-quarter guidance exceeded expectations. AMD (AMD.O) rose by 7.7%, Arm (ARM.O) gained 7.5%, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index increased by 3.7%. Amazon (AMZN.O) saw a 1.4% rise, following an agreement with Meta to use Amazon's Graviton CPU chips for AI development.
